evidence.claim:
PubMed ID: 11695769. 2001/10/01 00:00. PURPOSE: The GammaPod is a novel dedicated prone breast stereotactic 
radiosurgery (SRS) device recently developed at the University of Maryland 
Medical Center.
evidence.source_title: Reference dosimetry in clinical high-energy electron beams: comparison of the AAPM TG-51 and AAPM TG-21 dosimetry protocols.
evidence.raw_snippet:
PubMed ID: 11695769. 2001/10/01 00:00. PURPOSE: The GammaPod is a novel dedicated prone breast stereotactic 
radiosurgery (SRS) device recently developed at the University of Maryland 
Medical Center. This device utilizes multiple rotating Co-60 sources to create 
highly conformal dose distributions for breast treatments, including boosts, 
partial breast irradiation, or presurgery SRS. However, due to its small field 
sizes and nonstandard geometry, existing calibration protocols cannot be
